response.justification: The candidate is an experienced full-stack and systems software developer with over 15 years of experience, satisfying the years-of-experience bar for the Bachelor's path. However, his background is centered on web, mobile, and product engineering (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang), with no evidence of SoC or IP modeling, GPU or accelerator architecture, deep-learning workload analysis, or hardware-software co-design — the core requirements of this Principal Performance Architect role. The low-latency DLang work at Dunnhumby is the closest adjacency, but it does not translate to hardware performance architecture at the principal level.
strengths: Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (async I/O, fibers, caching at millions of requests/sec)
Broad polyglot background including C/C++ work on engineering tooling at National Instruments
15+ years of professional experience with a track record of ownership and open-source contribution
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, hardware architecture, or hardware-software co-design experience
No deep-learning, AI accelerator, GPU architecture, or SIMD/MIMD workload background
Career trajectory is web/full-stack product engineering, far from principal-level performance architecture

key_factors.factor: No SoC/IP modeling or hardware co-design experience
No AI/deep-learning or GPU workload expertise
